what_is_tested: Shared middle caption fragment should apply London location/date to multiple top-row photos
notes: Top row contains three London street photos. The typed strip reads 'OXFORD STREET   LONDON, ENGLAND - AUG. 1988   REGENT STREET'. The left and center photos should inherit the Oxford Street-specific label plus the shared London/date fragment. The right photo should inherit the Regent Street-specific label plus the same shared London/date fragment. This eval bypasses pipeline caching and calls LM Studio directly with the production page prompt and JSON schema.
pass_criteria: Model returns valid JSON matching the page-caption schema
OCR/author output preserves the shared middle fragment as distinct structure rather than flattening it into only one neighboring caption
Returned text clearly supports a month-level date of 1988-08 from 'AUG. 1988'
Result does not collapse into repeated garbage tokens or malformed JSON

input_context.ocr_text: OXFORD STREET LONDON,ENGLAND - AUG.1988 REGENT STREET OUR HOTEL IN ZAGREB,YUGOSLAVIA HOTEL INTER-CONTINENTAL AND THE MIMARA ART MUSEUM-AUGUST 1988

direct_model_result.json_parse_error: JSONDecodeError: Unterminated string starting at: line 1 column 14 (char 13)

observations: This direct call reproduced the failure without writing through the pipeline, so caching is not the root cause of the malformed page-caption output
The model failed before the semantic question of shared caption assignment could be evaluated; it did not produce valid structured JSON

The failure mode changed across runs: earlier pipeline runs showed malformed long strings, and a later streamed run hit an LM Studio runtime error; the direct non-streaming run still fails with runaway repeated tokens
Because the direct model output is malformed, prompt quality and model/runtime reliability must be separated from downstream XMP propagation or cache concerns
